hypothesis: The chemical template effect strength of a metal sublattice, as quantified by interstitial electron localization function maxima, will exhibit a systematic and transferable correlation with superconducting transition temperature across ternary metal superhydrides (M₁M₂Hₓ), such that ML models trained exclusively on binary superhydride metal lattices can predict stable ternary superhydride compositions with Tc > 150 K by identifying metal sublattice pairs whose combined template effects produce synergistic interstitial charge density distributions exceeding those achievable by either constituent metal alone.
